metadata.title: The Artificial Intelligence Crossroads: Build or Buy?
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nik Bear Brown
metadata.credit_line: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. TechServe runs three divisions, with Product Development at 280 employees, a third of total headcount. As Chief Transformation Officer you must choose to build or buy AI capability. StreamlineOS cut 400 support roles and automated 71% of queries, but only reported 8% cost reduction. Rival Glassdoor 'chaos' and 47 open Customer Success roles warn that cost savings hide Productivity J-Curve pain.

artifactLines: TechServe runs three divisions, with Product Development at 280 employees, a third of total headcount.
As Chief Transformation Officer you must choose to build or buy AI capability.
StreamlineOS cut 400 support roles and automated 71% of queries, but only reported 8% cost reduction.
Rival Glassdoor 'chaos' and 47 open Customer Success roles warn that cost savings hide Productivity J-Curve pain.

beats.narration_text: As TechServe's Chief Transformation Officer, I'm stuck on the build-or-buy call while StreamlineOS already cut 400 support roles for an 8% cost drop yet shows internal 'chaos' and 47 open Customer Success jobs. I want to reason past headline savings into the Productivity J-Curve dip. Walk me through: (1) how to model the near-term productivity trough before gains appear; (2) three metrics beyond cost savings that prove I'm navigating it well; (3) whether Product Development's 280 people, 33% of headcount, argue for build over buy.
